Title: A Thylakoid Membrane Protein Functions Synergistically with GUN5 in Chlorophyll Biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Thylakoid membrane protein with six putative transmembrane domains; C-terminal region contains motifs characteristic of type II CAAX prenyl proteases
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential for photosynthetic light harvesting and chloroplast development
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Specifically expressed in green tissues; induced by light; synchronized diurnal expression with CHLH/GUN5
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Specifically expressed in green tissues including leaves, stems, flowers, and immature siliques; not detected in roots; induced during de-etiolation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Light exposure during de-etiolation; dark adaptation; ALA feeding; low-Mg conditions in Salmonella complementation assay
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout mutants (cbd1-1, cbd1-2); genomic complementation; chlorophyll quantification; de-etiolation assay; metabolite spectrofluorometry of Mg-Proto IX/Mg-Proto IX ME; double mutant analysis with gun5 and chlm; ICP-MS metal analysis; heterologous complementation in Mg2+-uptake-deficient Salmonella MM281
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of CBD1 reduces chlorophyll accumulation, increases chlorophyll a/b ratio, causes pale-green leaves, leads to over-accumulation of Mg-Proto IX/Mg-Proto IX ME after ALA feeding, lowers thylakoid Mg content, and aggravates chlorosis with gun5
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CBD1 is a previously uncharacterized thylakoid membrane protein specifically expressed in green tissues and tightly co-expressed with chlorophyll biosynthetic genes, especially CHLH/GUN5. 2. cbd1 loss-of-function mutants show reduced total chlorophyll, slower greening, and accumulation of Mg-Proto IX/Mg-Proto IX ME, indicating impairment in Mg-Proto IX processing. 3. cbd1gun5 double mutants display additive chlorophyll deficiency and severe chloroplast/thylakoid defects, supporting synergistic action with GUN5. 4. CBD1 lowers/maintains chloroplast Mg homeostasis and can complement a Mg2+ uptake-deficient Salmonella strain under low Mg, supporting a Mg transport/homeostasis role linked to chlorophyll biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function causes chlorosis, reduced chlorophyll, stunted growth in combination with gun5, chloroplast developmental defects, and altered assembly of photosynthetic super-complexes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CBD1 is identical to BCM1 reported independently; authors propose CBD1 functions upstream of CHLM and may traffic Mg-Proto IX to the methylation step and/or act as a thylakoid Mg transporter/anchor rather than as a catalytic pathway enzyme
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CBD1 native promoter (tissue-expression assay); 35S promoter (CBD1-GFP localization)
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Co-expression analysis using Genevestigator database; no new omics accession reported

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as the catalytic substrate-binding subunit of magnesium chelatase to insert Mg2+ into protoporphyrin IX, generating Mg-protoporphyrin IX
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Mg-chelation and Mg-Proto IX processing module of tetrapyrrole biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed/early Mg-branch step with additional role in transfer to the next methylation step
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of chlorophyll and promotes downstream processing toward chlorophyll
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential for photosynthetic light harvesting and chloroplast development
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Light-inducible and diurnally regulated; synchronized with CBD1
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Light-inducible and diurnally regulated in seedlings/plants studied
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dark adaptation; ALA feeding; de-etiolation/light exposure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gun5 point mutant analysis; double mutant analysis with cbd1; chlorophyll measurement; spectrofluorometric detection of Mg-Proto IX/Mg-Proto IX ME; chloroplast ultrastructure analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: gun5 reduces chlorophyll content and, together with cbd1, causes stronger chlorosis and defects in Mg-Proto IX processing and chloroplast development
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step in Mg-branch chlorophyll biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GUN5/CHLH is a core Mg-chelatase catalytic subunit required for Mg-Proto IX formation. 2. gun5 mutants have reduced chlorophyll and reduced Mg-Proto IX. 3. In combination with cbd1, GUN5 deficiency causes severe chlorosis and marked accumulation of Mg-Proto IX/Mg-Proto IX ME, indicating synergistic function with CBD1 in downstream Mg-Proto IX processing/trafficking.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced function causes chlorosis, impaired chloroplast development, and disrupted photosynthetic complex assembly
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Besides Mg-chelation, CHLH/GUN5 is discussed as enhancing MgMT/CHLM catalysis by channeling Mg-Proto IX; also known from retrograde signaling and ABA response, but these were background roles in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genevestigator co-expression analysis; no accession reported

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates the propionate side chain of Mg-protoporphyrin IX to form Mg-protoporphyrin IX monomethyl ester
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Mg-Proto IX methylation step of tetrapyrrole biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate downstream intermediate toward protochlorophyllide and chlorophyll
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential for photosynthetic light harvesting and chloroplast development
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Dark adaptation and ALA feeding for intermediate measurements
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Reference mutant chlm and cbd1chlm double mutant genetic comparison; metabolite spectrofluorometry
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: chlm accumulates Mg-Proto IX, and cbd1chlm phenocopies chlm, supporting that CBD1 acts upstream of CHLM-dependent methylation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHLM methylates Mg-Proto IX to Mg-Proto IX monomethyl ester in chlorophyll biosynthesis. 2. chlm was used as a pathway reference showing increased Mg-Proto IX accumulation. 3. cbd1chlm resembling chlm supports the conclusion that CBD1 acts upstream of CHLM and affects substrate delivery/processing to the methylation step.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation blocks Mg-Proto IX methylation and impairs chlorophyll accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a primary discovery gene in this paper, but directly used in Results as a reference mutant to position CBD1 within the pathway
